prevalence.value: 10-20% annual incidence in adult women (symptomatic episodes)
prevalence.source: European primary care epidemiology syntheses / EAU context
prevalence.trend: stable; resistance patterns vary by country

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Symptom-based diagnosis for uncomplicated cystitis with selective urinalysis/culture; culture for complicated or recurrent cases; EAU-aligned guidance

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: no screening (pregnancy bacteriuria screening policies vary)
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: High cure rates; stewardship focuses on resistance reduction

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Strong primary care pathways and culture availability for complicated cases
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Nitrofurantoin/fosfomycin first-line in many guidelines; avoid fluoroquinolones for uncomplicated infections; stewardship and resistance surveillance programs
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Rising ESBL prevalence in some countries and stewardship implementation variability
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: EAU guidance and robust antimicrobial stewardship in many member states
